Title: Genome-based discovery of pachysiphine synthases in Tabernaemontana elegans

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive transient expression via pHREAC in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves for activity assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No detectable N-methylation of tabersonine or pachysiphine under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana with tabersonine or pachysiphine feeding; LC-MS analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding with tabersonine or pachysiphine
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1124898
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Currently limited direct value for pachysiphine engineering; may act on other MIA substrates and warrants future testing
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tel01G.16930 is a PYS2-neighboring putative N-methyltransferase candidate. 2. It showed no activity toward tabersonine or pachysiphine in the assay used. 3. The study suggests it may target other MIAs rather than these tested substrates.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was directly functionally tested in Results, but negative on focal substrates.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP71D family (T3O-like subfamily); N-terminal transmembrane helix
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Tabernaemontana elegans; Nicotiana benthamiana; Catharanthus roseus; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Tabernaemontana elegans; Nicotiana benthamiana; Catharanthus roseus; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive plant transient expression via pHREAC in N. benthamiana and C. roseus; constitutive yeast expression in donor vectors (specific promoters not stated)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: RNA-seq from immature leaf, mature leaf, stem, flower bud, flower, and root; detailed gene-specific pattern referenced in Figure S3 but not stated in text
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves; overexpressed in young C. roseus leaves; constitutively expressed in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tabersonine oxygenation branch leading to pachysiphine
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Tabersonine; also 16-hydroxytabersonine; 16-methoxytabersonine; low activity on 19-hydroxytabersonine in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Pachysiphine; also pachysiphine derivatives from accepted substrates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epoxidizes the C6–C7 double bond of tabersonine in a specific orientation to form pachysiphine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Occurs in Tabernaemontana species; specific organ not explicitly stated for pachysiphine in T. elegans
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pachysiphine accumulation and redirects tabersonine flux toward pachysiphine and downstream methoxylated derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana with tabersonine feeding; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; milligram-scale product purification; NMR structural confirmation; transient overexpression in C. roseus; yeast pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding with tabersonine or derivatives; heterologous expression in tobacco, roseus leaves, and yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1124898
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported; substrate scope excludes 2,3-epoxytabersonine and largely excludes 19-hydroxylated/19-acetylated substrates, constraining pathway combinations
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme for microbial or plant metabolic engineering of pachysiphine and new-to-nature derivatives; useful for tabersonine bioconversion and gene stacking with T16H/16OMT/T19H/TAT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tel01G.15088 encodes a bona fide pachysiphine synthase designated PYS1. 2. PYS1 converts tabersonine to pachysiphine by C6–C7 epoxidation, confirmed by LC-MS/MS and NMR. 3. PYS1 functions in planta and in yeast to produce pachysiphine and enables synthesis of new-to-nature derivatives when combined with other pathway enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tel01G.15088. T3O-like rather than TEX-like evolutionary origin; docking/modeling suggest substrate orientation and channel architecture determine regio- and stereoselectivity.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP71D family (T3O-like subfamily); N-terminal transmembrane helix
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Tabernaemontana elegans; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Tabernaemontana elegans; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive plant transient expression via pHREAC in N. benthamiana; constitutive yeast expression in donor vectors (specific promoters not stated)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: RNA-seq from multiple organs; detailed gene-specific pattern referenced in Figure S3 but not stated in text
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in tobacco leaves; expressed in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Tabersonine oxygenation branch leading to pachysiphine
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Tabersonine; also 16-hydroxytabersonine; 16-methoxytabersonine; low activity on 19-hydroxytabersonine in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Pachysiphine; also pachysiphine derivatives from accepted substrates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epoxidizes the C6–C7 double bond of tabersonine in a specific orientation to form pachysiphine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Occurs in Tabernaemontana species; specific organ not explicitly stated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pachysiphine formation from tabersonine in heterologous assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana with tabersonine feeding; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; comparative modeling and docking; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding with tabersonine or derivatives in tobacco and yeast systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1124898
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported; limited acceptance of 19-modified and 2,3-epoxidized substrates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative pachysiphine synthase for metabolic engineering and enzyme diversification studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tel01G.16933 encodes a second pachysiphine synthase designated PYS2. 2. PYS2 converts tabersonine to pachysiphine in transient assays. 3. PYS2 is ER-localized and highly similar to PYS1, supporting parallel engineering use and evolutionary analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tel01G.16933. Located in syntenic region containing PYS2 paralogs and a nearby putative NMT gene.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive transient expression via pHREAC in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient co-expression in tobacco leaves for OMT screening
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid / tabersonine decoration pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates tabersonine at C16 to form 16-hydroxytabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies 16-hydroxylated precursor used to identify and functionally validate Te16OMTs and enables methoxylated pachysiphine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/MS comparison to authentic 16-hydroxytabersonine standard; co-expression with OMT candidates
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1124898
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering 16-hydroxylated and methoxylated tabersonine/pachysiphine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tel01G.21745 was identified as a bona fide tabersonine 16-hydroxylase. 2. The enzyme produces 16-hydroxytabersonine from tabersonine in tobacco assays. 3. TeT16H enabled downstream screening of Te16OMT enzymes and pathway reconstruction toward methoxylated derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tel01G.21745; initially recovered in TEX-like screen but product matched 16-hydroxytabersonine rather than pachysiphine.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive transient expression via pHREAC in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently co-expressed in tobacco with TeT16H
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid / O-methylation of tabersonine-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of methoxylated tabersonine and pachysiphine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with TeT16H in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/MS comparison with authentic 16-methoxytabersonine standard
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1124898
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering 16-methoxy MIA derivatives, including new-to-nature pachysiphine analogs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tel01G.8582 encodes an active 16-O-methyltransferase named Te16OMT1. 2. It converts 16-hydroxytabersonine to 16-methoxytabersonine. 3. It also methylates 16-hydroxypachysiphine, expanding pachysiphine derivative diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three active neighboring OMT paralogs likely generated by local duplication.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive transient expression via pHREAC in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently co-expressed in tobacco with TeT16H
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid / O-methylation of tabersonine-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of methoxylated tabersonine and pachysiphine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with TeT16H in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/MS comparison with authentic 16-methoxytabersonine standard
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1124898
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for combinatorial pathway engineering of methoxylated pachysiphine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tel01G.8583 encodes an active 16-O-methyltransferase named Te16OMT2. 2. It methylates 16-hydroxytabersonine to 16-methoxytabersonine. 3. It also accepts 16-hydroxypachysiphine as substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Close genomic neighbor of Te16OMT1 and Te16OMT3.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive transient expression via pHREAC in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently co-expressed in tobacco with TeT16H
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid / O-methylation of tabersonine-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of methoxylated tabersonine and pachysiphine derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with TeT16H in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/MS comparison with authentic 16-methoxytabersonine standard
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1124898
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway diversification and metabolic engineering of 16-methoxy MIAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tel01G.8584 encodes an active 16-O-methyltransferase named Te16OMT3. 2. It forms 16-methoxytabersonine from 16-hydroxytabersonine. 3. It also methylates 16-hydroxypachysiphine, indicating substrate flexibility.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Third active paralog in a locally duplicated OMT cluster.
